pattern: Dynamic programming that tracks, at every cell, how many down-right paths arrive with each possible running-sum remainder modulo k.
hints: Only the path sum's remainder modulo k matters, so carry a count for each of the k remainders into every cell.
A cell inherits path-remainder counts from the cell above and to the left, then shifts every remainder by the cell's own value.
Let dp[i][j][r] be the number of paths reaching (i,j) whose sum mod k is r; combine the two predecessors, rotate by grid[i][j], and read dp[m-1][n-1][0] modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: single-row single-cell pair with zero sum
k equals one so every path counts

stress.description: random small rectangular grid so the exponential oracle stays fast while many paths overlap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 6)))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 6)))
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 100) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 50)
    return [grid, k]
stress.note: bounded to <=6x6 so the brute-force path enumeration terminates quickly
